reviews.review_description:
Amazing. This is how stories need be told.
Please make more experiences.
Thanks

developer_response.body: Hi tkansal, thanks for the kind review! If you haven't already, may we recommend you try out "Anne Frank House VR"? Thanks and have a great day.

reviews.review_description: You cannot help but feel you have met Rembrandt. This is an exceptional VR experience. Highly recommended
Ok it is not a game and not very interactive but it takes Gearvr graphics and immersion to a whole new level. Beautiful.
A short, but outstanding experience. The graphics are really good!
One of the first experiences I've seen to blend live acting with CGI environments, and it's well done. Hope to see more from this developer. Would love more of these historic European environments
A cool time machine. Impressive design. Sadly no ability to pause, skip or rewind.

This is a stereoscopic (aka "3D"), 360° video mixing CGI and live actors presenting a couple of small scenes featuring Rembrandt. It has at least one "interactive" scene, so you can watch it at least two times and have slightly different outcomes.

I really like this VR experience, and I would love to see expansion to make it longer, like additional conversations, more interactions that could change the outcome, and maybe additional environments.  My only complaint is the game is far too short. Great job.
Its really great to watch...amazing affects and true vr experience
Wonderful way for learning about history of art. Expected to have some interactions with the controller but didn't find any.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your support!  You are correct, there is no controller interaction.  The only interactive element is the head turning.  It tracks what you do...Rembrandt will react differently depending on if you do what he says or not!

reviews.review_title: Well done, but could be more educational
reviews.review_description:
This was well executed.  Its shirt, but you get a small glimpse into Rembrandts life.  The combo of video and graphics is well done and the responses from the characters if you dont follow their instructions is spot on.

Only complaints is that it's quite short and it didn't go into detail more than one specific painting.

However, I used this to show my Mom VR and so she could try something. She is a hemipalegic and since it only required her to look around, not use a remote or press buttons once I got it started, she was able to do it.  She smiled, turned her head, and made audible sounds. All of which are really rare.  Just wish it was longer.
As a frequent museum goer I didn’t expect much but it was actually very enjoyable! Thank you to the creators of this great experience
